summary: A small rectangular two-storey Minecraft building with a stepped brick gable roof, pale yellow sandstone walls banded by white smooth stone trim, narrow horizontal windows on both floors, and a low white stone border/walkway around the base. The roof ridge runs along the long axis; building sits centered in a shallow dirt excavation.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular block with a pitched stepped roof
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along long axis (mirrored front/back and left/right about centerline)

elements: rows of narrow horizontal windows on both floors (aligned)
stepped/terraced brick gable roof with stone slab/trim at eaves
white stone (quartz) corner pillars and horizontal trim between floors
low white stone border around base (1 block high)
rebuild_hints: Footprint is rectangular with long axis under the roof ridge; center the gable ridge along long axis.
Walls: two stacked horizontal bands of sandstone as main wall with a continuous white trim band (quartz) between floors and white vertical corner blocks.
Windows: place evenly spaced 1x2 (or 1x1 high narrow) horizontal glass openings in continuous rows on each floor wrapping the long facades; maintain same spacing both floors.
Roof: construct a symmetrical stepped brick gable roof (stair-like steps) rising to a short ridge, add stone slab trim at eaves/shoulders as seen.
Surrounding: a one-block-high white stone (quartz) border/walk encloses the building footprint with a dirt excavation around it.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone vs chiseled/smooth variants not distinguishable; quartz assumed for white trim but could be smooth stone or slab_stone.
Window exact dimensions and spacing: appear as narrow continuous horizontal gaps; exact height (1 vs 2 blocks) uncertain but likely 1-block high strips.
Internal layout and presence/location of entrance(s) not visible in provided views.
Precise building footprint dimensions estimated from images; could be off by a few blocks (±2).
Height estimate: includes two floors plus roof; floor heights per storey are inferred from window placement and may vary.
